subject: Project Management
title: Flight Levels
description: A quick, practical guide to Flight Levels.

hook.text: Klaus Leopold introduced 'Flight Levels' — applying Kanban at team, coordination, and strategy.

buildup.text: Level 1: a single team's board. Level 2: multiple teams coordinating. Level 3: portfolio flow.

discovery.text: Most organizations optimize individual teams but ignore the connections between them.

twist.text: Local optimization often hurts global flow. A fast team feeding a slow one just builds a queue.

climax.text: Flight Levels show that real improvement requires managing flow across the whole system.

punchline.text: Fast teams don't help if the system is slow.

quiz.question: What does Flight Level 3 in Kanban represent?
